title: Sinners Costume Design by Ruth E. Carter: Oscars 2026 Nominees
text: Ruth E. Carter earned her fifth Academy Award nomination for her costume design work on Ryan Coogler's Sinners, a film set in the Mississippi Delta during the early 1930s that explores themes of race, identity, and cultural resilience. Carter drew inspiration from American paintings to develop a red, white, and blue color scheme. She researched historical sources including photography by Eudora Welty and paintings by Romare Bearden, Jacob Lawrence, and Norman Rockwell to authentically represent the period. The twin characters Smoke and Stack received distinct color treatments. Stack wears red to reflect his transformation after time in Chicago, suggesting a passionate, self-conscious personality. Smoke wears blue in a more relaxed, boxy grey suit without a tie, representing the everyman who cares less about appearance. Annie received spiritual styling with a raw silk fringed skirt with sequins and beaded prayer necklaces. Carter incorporated Haint Blue, a traditional Southern shade believed to ward off spirits, throughout Annie's wardrobe. Mary was dressed in pale pink to suggest she's ghostly and disconnected from her community after marrying wealth. For the juke joint scene, Carter incorporated 1990s hip-hop influences, including custom Adidas Gazelles and elaborate pieces like a West African Zulu dancer outfit featuring a mask created from Ankara strips dyed dark purple.
